vBRIEFInfo.author: planning-agent:claude-opus-4-5
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1044: Command Deck — Project Overview Panel with pipeline swimlanes. Empirically scoped 2026-05-09 (revised with cost-breakdown-tooltip bead and cost-on-stuck-callout) — see docs/prds/planned/PAN-1044-project-overview-panel.md.

plan.title: Command Deck: Project Overview Panel with pipeline swimlanes
plan.author: planning-agent:claude-opus-4-5

narratives.Problem: When clicking a project name in the Command Deck sidebar, the only behavior is toggling expand/collapse. There is no way to see a holistic view of a project — how many issues are active, where they sit in the pipeline, which are stuck, and what the aggregate and per-issue cost is.
narratives.Proposal: Add a Project Overview panel in the Command Deck right content area. Clicking a project name (not the chevron) selects the project and shows: a hero billboard with aggregate stats, a stuck/blocked callout, and pipeline swimlanes bucketing every issue by its current stage. Each issue card shows its cost, and hovering the cost badge reveals a popover with per-model and per-stage cost breakdown. All data is client-side — no new API endpoints. One new bead (cost-breakdown-tooltip) handles the tooltip independently from the core swimlane layout.

narrative.Action: File docs/prds/planned/PAN-1044-project-overview-panel.md already created by the previous planning pass. Verify the file exists and contains the codebase corrections (SessionNode.type not role, Record not Map, uppercase issueId). Mark bead complete.

subItems.title: File exists at docs/prds/planned/PAN-1044-project-overview-panel.md
Codebase corrections documented (SessionNode.type, Record types, uppercase issueId)

narrative.Action: Create ProjectOverview.tsx with: (1) exported bucketFeature(feature, reviewStatus) function returning PipelineStage — bucketing order: stuck (via reviewStatus.stuck, failed statuses, blockerReasons), merging, awaitingMerge, tests, review, buildGate, working (agentStatus !== null && !reviewStatus), planning (hasPlanning && no s.type==='work' session), idle. Use s.type === 'work' NOT s.role. Export PipelineStage type. (2) ProjectOverviewProps: { projectName: string; features: ProjectFeature[]; issueCosts: Record<string, number>; issueCostDetails?: Record<string, { byModel: Record<string, { cost: number; tokens: number }>; byStage: Record<string, { cost: number; tokens: number }> }>; onSelectFeature: (feature: ProjectFeature) => void }. (3) Hero billboard: background linear-gradient(135deg, color-mix(in srgb, var(--primary) 8%, transparent), transparent), project name 18px weight 700, issue count, total cost via LiveCounter, active agent count. (4) useDashboardStore(s => s.reviewStatusByIssueId) at top of component — NOT in a loop. Create ProjectOverview.test.tsx covering all 9 bucketFeature stage branches: stuck via stuck flag, stuck via failed status, stuck via blockerReasons, merging, awaitingMerge, tests, review, buildGate, working, planning, idle.

subItems.title: ProjectOverview.tsx renders hero billboard with project name, issue count, total cost (LiveCounter), active agent count
bucketFeature exported, returns correct PipelineStage for all 9 inputs, uses s.type === 'work'
ProjectOverview.test.tsx covers all 9 stage branches of bucketFeature
issueCostDetails is an optional prop on ProjectOverviewProps (can be undefined — no tooltip yet)
npm run typecheck and npm test pass

items.title: Pipeline swimlanes + stuck/blocked callout with per-issue cost badges

narrative.Action: Complete ProjectOverview.tsx with stuck callout and pipeline swimlanes: (1) Stuck/blocked callout: AlertTriangle icon, var(--destructive) accent, rendered only when stuck issues exist. Per stuck card: Issue ID (clickable onSelectFeature), title truncated to 1 line, stuck reason (stuckReason or blockerReasons[0] or failed status name), PLUS a cost badge showing the issue cost when issueCosts[feature.issueId] exists — consistent with swimlane cards. (2) Pipeline swimlane sections: one Section per non-empty stage. Header: stage name + count badge using the stage color token (var(--destructive)/var(--info)/var(--success)/var(--signal-review)/var(--warning)/var(--primary)/var(--muted-foreground) per PRD). Body: horizontal flex-wrap of issue cards. Per swimlane card: Issue ID (clickable), title truncated (12px), cost badge showing issueCosts[feature.issueId] when present, sub-status pill (review: reviewSubStatuses roles; merge: mergeStep; buildGate: verificationCycleCount if > 1). Empty stages hidden. ALL colors via CSS variables only — no hardcoded hex or rgb values. The cost badge in both stuck callout and swimlane cards is the same element that the cost-breakdown-tooltip bead (next) will add hover behavior to.

subItems.title: Stuck/blocked callout appears only when stuck issues exist, with AlertTriangle icon and var(--destructive) accent
Stuck callout cards show cost badge (same as swimlane cards) when issueCosts entry exists
Pipeline swimlane sections rendered, one per non-empty stage, with correct stage color token
Empty pipeline stages are hidden
Swimlane cards show cost badge when issueCosts entry exists
Clicking issue card in any swimlane or stuck callout calls onSelectFeature with correct feature
All colors use CSS variables only (no hardcoded values)
npm run typecheck and npm test pass

items.title: ProjectOverview — cost badge hover tooltip with model/stage breakdown

narrative.Action: Add inline CostBreakdownTooltip component to ProjectOverview.tsx. Use the same hover-popover pattern as FeatureItem.tsx: onMouseEnter={() => setPopoverOpen(true)}, onMouseLeave={() => setPopoverOpen(false)}, onFocus/onBlur for keyboard accessibility. The cost badge in both stuck callout cards and swimlane cards becomes the hover target. When issueCostDetails[feature.issueId] is available: show a CSS-positioned popover panel containing (1) model rows sorted by cost desc — model name (friendly display, strip 'claude-' prefix and date suffix) + formatted cost '$X.XX'; (2) stage rows sorted by cost desc — stage label (use same STAGE_CONFIG labels as CostBreakdownModal.tsx: planning/implementation/review/testing/merge/interactive/other) + formatted cost. Use CSS variables only. Graceful degradation: if issueCostDetails is absent or the specific issue has no entry, cost badge still renders normally with no hover behavior. Tooltip must reflect live data: since issueCostDetails is a prop derived from costData (refetched every 15s in CommandDeck), tooltip rows update automatically when the prop changes — no additional local state or fetch needed.

subItems.title: Hovering the cost badge opens a popover showing model rows sorted by cost desc
Popover also shows stage rows sorted by cost desc with friendly stage labels
Mouse-leave closes the popover
When issueCostDetails is absent or has no entry for the issue, cost badge still renders with no tooltip
Tooltip data updates automatically when issueCostDetails prop changes (no stale snapshot)
Hover pattern matches FeatureItem.tsx resource popover (onMouseEnter/onMouseLeave/onFocus/onBlur)
npm run typecheck and npm test pass

items.title: ProjectNode.tsx — split click behavior and selection visual

narrative.Action: Modify ProjectTree/ProjectNode.tsx: (1) Add props: onSelectProject?: (projectName: string) => void and selectedProject?: string | null. (2) Split click targets: wrap ChevronRight in <span onClick={(e) => { e.stopPropagation(); setExpanded(!expanded); }} style={{ cursor: 'pointer', display: 'inline-flex', alignItems: 'center' }}>. Outer button onClick: calls onSelectProject?.(name) AND if (!expanded) setExpanded(true). (3) Apply style={{ background: selectedProject === name ? 'var(--accent)' : undefined }} to the projectHeader button. (4) The MessageSquarePlus span already uses e.stopPropagation() — preserve it exactly, do not touch it.

subItems.title: Clicking the ChevronRight span only toggles expand/collapse, does NOT call onSelectProject
Clicking the project name row calls onSelectProject and expands if collapsed
Selected project row shows var(--accent) background
MessageSquarePlus button still works and e.stopPropagation() is preserved
npm run typecheck passes

items.title: CommandDeck/index.tsx — selectedProject state, four-way routing, IssueCostEntry expansion, issueCostDetails wiring

narrative.Action: Modify CommandDeck/index.tsx: (1) Add state: const [selectedProject, setSelectedProject] = useState<string | null>(null). (2) Add handler: const handleSelectProject = useCallback((projectName: string) => { setSelectedProject(projectName); setSelectedFeature(null); setSelectedConversation(null); }, []). (3) Update handleSelectFeature to add setSelectedProject(null). (4) Update handleSelectConversation to add setSelectedProject(null). (5) Pass onSelectProject={handleSelectProject} and selectedProject={selectedProject} to each ProjectNode. (6) Expand IssueCostEntry interface to add byModel: Record<string, { cost: number; tokens: number }> and byStage: Record<string, { cost: number; tokens: number }>. (7) Build issueCostDetails: Record<string, { byModel: ..., byStage: ... }> from costData.issues — same memo that builds issueCosts, just capture the full detail too. (8) In content area routing, implement four-way priority: conversation → ConversationView, feature → IssueWorkbench, project → ProjectOverview (projectName={selectedProject}, features={projectsWithSessions.find(p => p.name === selectedProject)?.features ?? []}, issueCosts={issueCosts}, issueCostDetails={issueCostDetails}, onSelectFeature={feature => handleSelectFeature(feature.issueId)}), empty → EmptyState. Import ProjectOverview from './ProjectOverview'.

subItems.title: Selecting a project shows ProjectOverview in the content area
Selecting a feature clears selectedProject
Selecting a conversation clears selectedProject
Four-way routing priority: conversation > feature > project > empty
Clicking issue card in ProjectOverview navigates to that issue's IssueWorkbench
IssueCostEntry includes byModel and byStage; issueCostDetails map is built from costData and passed to ProjectOverview
npm run typecheck, npm run lint, and npm test all pass
